name: How to build a network your future self won't hate
description:
Building a network that scales with your platform shouldn't mean choosing between simplicity and security. In this session, Tailscale solutions engineer Jay Stapleton walks through the principles of scalable network design covering segmentation, zero-trust access, and patterns that stay manageable as your infrastructure grows.

Join Jay to discover:

1. How to design a network architecture that scales with your platform without becoming a maintenance burden - including key principles around segmentation and access control.
2. How zero-trust networking works in practice and why it's become a foundational pattern for platform and DevOps teams managing hybrid or multi-cloud environments.
3. How to evaluate tooling decisions that balance developer experience, security, and operational simplicity at scale.

The ICS Advisory Project (ICSAP) launched in 2022 as a free vulnerability intelligence resource for the OT/ICS security community.

Four years and 12,000+ curated advisories later, founder Dan Ricci is building a commercial Docker product alongside the free community offering. Drawing on 30
years of cybersecurity experience spanning Navy cyber operations, defense industry product security, and DOE-funded national lab research, Dan shares the engineering and product decisions behind that evolution - how AI-augmented development is reshaping the weekly editorial workflow, what asset owners actually need from an on-premises OT vuln intel platform, and how he's keeping the free community resource healthy alongside a sustainable commercial offering.

name: ResearchersConnect: Global Virtual Job & Networking Event for Researchers
description:
Are you a PhD student, postdoc, faculty member, or research professional looking to grow your academic network, find job opportunities, or explore potential collaborations? **ResearchersConnect** is your new go-to **virtual research networking event**, designed to bring together minds from across disciplines, institutions, and geographies.
Hosted on a Slack-like platform, this **online community for researchers** offers a space for idea exchange, knowledge sharing, and career development. Whether you're seeking a new role, scouting research collaborators, or learning about the latest **research funding and grants**, this platform empowers you to connect meaningfully — all from the comfort of your home or office.

🎥 **Watch the explainer video to see how to make the most of your experience:**
👉 [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K0PaumQ9Rj8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K0PaumQ9Rj8)

**💬 What You Can Expect:**
Join us in a flexible and engaging **academic career fair online**, where participants have access to multiple themed channels and direct 1-1 video or text conversations.
Here’s how the space is organized to help you thrive:

* **General** – Your welcome lounge for intros, announcements, and community-wide discussions. Great for getting oriented and starting conversations.
* **Intros** – Share your academic background, research focus, or current project. Connect with like-minded individuals.
* **Networking** – Post your portfolio, recent publications, or research goals. Explore interdisciplinary connections and brainstorm with fellow attendees.
* **Help Wanted** – Looking for a co-author, lab partner, or someone with statistical expertise? Post your ask or offer help here.
* **Research Funding & Grants** – Exchange tips, resources, and experiences around securing research funding, writing proposals, or navigating fellowship applications.
* **Collaboration Corner** – Meet potential partners for cross-disciplinary research and innovative joint ventures.
* **Industry Room: Research & Academia** – Stay up to date on trends, methodologies, and tools shaping the future of research in academia and industry.

You’ll be able to:

* Chat live with fellow attendees across the globe
* Join topic-specific channels tailored to your goals
* Conduct **1-on-1 video chats** with researchers, hiring professionals, and peers
* Share resources, publications, and job openings with the community
* Explore a vibrant **research collaboration platform** that fosters real-world impact

**📍 Location:**
All sessions take place virtually at
👉 [https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/6wmav4fgzzha](https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/6wmav4fgzzha)

**🌍 Who Should Join?**

* Graduate students and postdocs seeking their next academic or industry role
* Professors and principal investigators open to new projects or interdisciplinary connections
* Research professionals and employers scouting talent or collaborators
* Grant writers and administrators looking for strategic partnerships
* Anyone involved in **academic research, scientific collaboration, or proposal development**

**🕓 When:**
The event runs from **4:00 PM – 6:00 PM local time** in your region. Visit the event link above for details and access.

**💡 Have Questions or Suggestions?**
We’d love to hear from you! Drop us a note here:
👉 [https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support](https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support)

name: How to architect Agentic Development Platforms
description:
Building an Agentic Development Platform requires integrating two fundamentally different system types: probabilistic components that generate and reason, and deterministic systems that validate, enforce, and govern. Getting this architecture right is the difference between agents that accelerate delivery and agents that introduce risk at scale.

Kaspar von Grünberg, Dilek Altin, and Ajay Chankramath a running a working session on how to architect an ADP, covering the Agent Plane, validation loop design, identity management for non-human actors, and the reference architecture patterns emerging from the field. Join us to learn:
- The core architectural distinction between probabilistic and deterministic systems in an ADP, and how the platform governs the interaction between them to make autonomous execution safe
- How to design the Agent Plane, the new probabilistic layer that sits above the existing deterministic platform substrate, including agent identity, context management, and sandboxed environment provisioning
- Reference architecture patterns for each level of agentic maturity, with practical guidance on what to build first and how to validate that the platform is ready to support the next level

name: Increasing Trust on Ethereum with ENS and Enscribe
description:
Most organizations on Ethereum are effectively anonymous. Contracts are deployed to nameless addresses, treasury wallets have no verifiable link to the org that controls them, and AI agents operate with no on-chain accountability. For protocols and DAOs trying to build credibility and operate transparently, this is a real problem — and it's one that existing tooling hasn't solved.

In this webinar, we'll walk through how organizations can easily create and manage on-chain identity for everything they deploy, using ENS as the identity layer supported by Enscribe ([https://www.enscribe.xyz/](https://www.enscribe.xyz/)). We'll show how protocols can name and manage their smart contracts, wallets, and agents under a unified namespace, making their on-chain presence verifiable, human-readable, and auditable.

name: Lineth Technical Deep Dive: Inside the Production-Grade ZK Rollup Stack
description:
With the announcement of Lineth ([https://github.com/LFDT-Lineth](https://github.com/LFDT-Lineth)) as a new Linux Foundation Decentralized Trust incubating project, we're opening the hood on the full stack in our first community Technical Deep Dive.

Lineth is a production-grade, EVM-equivalent zk-rollup stack for Ethereum - the same codebase that has powered Linea Mainnet since July 2023, finalizing over 300 million transactions and generating more than 416,000 zero-knowledge proofs with 99.98% sequencer uptime. It is now in a vendor-neutral, open source home under LFDT, governed by a Technical Steering Committee and with more than 30 project maintainers.

This session is your entry point into the architecture, the community, and the roadmap.

You'll leave with a practical diagnostic tool and a new way to position your work—and your value—inside organizations navigating change.

**About Beth:**
Beth Keller Kirycki is a UX researcher, content strategist, and information architect with 17 years of experience in high-stakes environments—education, healthcare, and mission-driven organizations—where precision, trust, and accessibility aren't nice to have, they're the whole point. Her work spans mixed-method research, information architecture, content governance, and AI-integrated workflows. She teaches professional writing and digital studies at Purdue University Fort Wayne, and is currently conducting IRB-approved research on empathy-driven communication in clinical settings.

name: Platform engineering 2.0: 5 pillars your platform needs in 2026 and beyond
description:
Platform engineering has crossed the adoption threshold. But the platforms most teams built weren't designed for AI workloads, autonomous agents, or a multi-persona organisation. In this session, Pankaj Gupta (Broadcom) and Sam Barlien (Platform Engineering) walk through the five pillars that define Platform Engineering 2.0: AI-native platform, multi-persona experience, embedded FinOps, security shifting down, and composable architecture. This is evolution, not revolution. But are you ready for it?

Join the discussion to learn:
1. Why today's IDPs have hit a structural ceiling and what the five forces driving that pressure are: AI-driven coding acceleration, autonomous agents, cloud and AI cost pressure, multi-persona organisations, and sovereignty regulation.
2. What each of the five pillars of Platform Engineering 2.0 requires in practice: AI-native infrastructure, embedded FinOps, security shifting into the platform layer, multi-persona experience, and composable architecture.
3. How to prioritise the evolution: which pillar to start with based on your most urgent pressure, and the 12-month milestone that matters most for AI readiness.

name: A Night At The Embassy of The European Union
description:
To secure your spot, purchase your ticket from the link ([https://www.eventbrite.com/e/a-night-at-the-european-union-embassydelegation-tickets-544451849137](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/a-night-at-the-european-union-embassydelegation-tickets-544451849137)). RSVPing only on the Meetup Page doesn't allow you to attend the Event. ADVANCE TICKET PURCHASE IS REQUIRED.

ABOUT THE EVENT

You are cordially invited to step on to the soil of the European Union, for a night filled with fun and information, on European culture, economy, business and tourism.

We meet, greet, network and socialize with European diplomats throughout the evening. Diplomats will deliver speeches on how the youth in Europe became environment conscious and what we could learn from that, here in the states.

RUNDOWN :

1. **BUFFET** : European finger food will be served.
2. **OPEN BAR** : European drinks, all night long.
3. **DRESS CODE** : Business/Cocktail.
4. **FORMAT** : This is primarily a stand up, buffet style reception, throughout the evening. However, a limited number of chairs will be around.
5. **TICKETS AVAILABILITY** : Very limited. Grab yours before we sell out. Also, prices are subject to increases as inventory declines.

**Payment Instructions :**

1\. You should login from a PC to see the 'Pay Now' button\, after RSVPing\. Then hit the button\.
2\. Verify correct dollar amount for number of people you're paying for\.
**3\. If you don't have a Paypal account\, click "Pay with a Debit or Credit Card"\.**
4\. If you have a Paypal account\, simply log in\, and pay as you normally would via Paypal\.

***How to Unblock the Pop-up Blocker on Your Phone***

Many of our patrons advised us that they have a Pop-up Blocker on their iPhones and it hinders them from buying tickets. To unblock, please follow the following steps :

For Safari Users :
1\. Go to Settings\.
2\. Scroll down to Safari and click on it\.
3\. Select Block Pop\-ups and turn it off\.

For Google Chrome Users :
1\. Open Google Chrome\.
2\. Click on the three dots and you will see many options\.
3\. Click on Settings\.
4\. Scroll down to content settings\.
5\. Select Block Pop\-ups and turn it off\.

REFUND AND TICKET RESELL POLICY

All sales are final. We only refund the full amount, if the event is canceled. If it is rescheduled, we will send an email to ticket holders advising that. A full refund will be given to those that let us know within 2 calendar days of the update email, if you are unable to make it on the new date. No refunds will be given after that time.

However, you can sell your ticket to someone else if you are unable to attend. Please, feel free to post in the comment box for the event, that you have a ticket for sale. Work out payment between yourselves, and have the seller only send the organizer a message letting us know the full name of the person taking your spot. You must let us know two hours before the RSVP close time, on the day of the event.

**\*\*FOR FURTHER INFO, REFER THE EVENTBRITE LINK AND PURCHASE TICKETS. IT RULES.\*\***
